text: A database is an organized collection of information stored electronically. There are two main types: SQL (relational) and NoSQL (non-relational) databases. SQL databases use tables with rows and columns, much like a spreadsheet. You retrieve data using queries, which are commands written in the SQL language. Good data modeling involves designing these tables to avoid data repetition. NoSQL databases, on the other hand, are more flexible and can store data as documents, key-value pairs, or graphs. This makes them suitable for unstructured data. To speed up queries, both types often use indexes, which help the database find information quickly without scanning every record.
